title: Longevity Vacations Are Trending in 2026 -- A Wellness Writer Reviews
description: Travel experts explain why longevity vacations are surging in popularity, with wellness resorts offering advanced diagnostics, cold plunges, and hyperbaric oxygen as vacation packages.

title: Telomere Transfer Preprint Claims Exceptional Lifespan Extension in Mice
description: A preprint from Sentcell reports that CD4+ T cells can transfer telomeres to other cells, producing dramatic lifespan extension in mice via a novel rejuvenation mechanism.

title: Bryan Johnson Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update Analysis
description: Detailed analysis of Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint update, which shifts from rigid calorie restriction toward adaptive, data-driven biomarker tracking and evidence-based refinement.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising in 2026
description: The five most-funded longevity startups (Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, Insilico Medicine) account for roughly $6.9 billion, with the top 10 capturing 67% of all sector funding.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033
description: Market analysis projects the longevity biotech sector will grow at a 20.4% CAGR from 2026 to 2033, driven by AI integration, genomics, and regenerative medicine advances.

title: Immorta Bio Demonstrates Dramatic Lifespan Extension in Aging Mouse Models
description: Immorta Bio's combination of SenoVax vaccine and StemCellRevivify stem cell therapy doubled lifespan in an accelerated-aging mouse model while reducing SASP inflammatory markers.

title: Scientists Successfully Freeze and Rewarm Mouse Brain Slices with Preserved Function
description: Researchers vitrified mouse brain slices and a complete brain, finding that much of the neuronal function was preserved upon rewarming -- a milestone for cryopreservation science.

title: ARPA-H Devotes $144 Million to Studies That Slow Aging and Extend Quality of Life
description: The PROSPR program awarded $144M to seven teams to build the framework for phase 3 clinical trials of interventions intended to increase human healthspan.

title: COSMOS Trial: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by ~4 Months Over 2 Years
description: A large randomized trial published in Nature Medicine found that a daily multivitamin slowed epigenetic aging by about four months over two years, with greater benefits for the biologically older.

title: COSMOS Trial Data: Mass General Brigham Details Multivitamin Aging Results
description: Mass General Brigham researchers detail how five epigenetic clock measures showed slowed biological aging in 958 participants taking a daily multivitamin in the COSMOS study.

title: A Review of How the Heart Ages
description: The European Heart Journal published a comprehensive review of cardiac aging, covering both cellular mechanisms and visible structural changes in the aging heart.

title: Fight Aging! Newsletter March 9 -- ARPA-H Funding, Gut Microbiome, Epigenetic Clocks
description: Weekly roundup covers ARPA-H clinical trial funding, gut microbiome changes driving intestinal barrier dysfunction, epigenetic clocks correlating with dementia risk, and mitochondrial function.

title: Daymond John's Biohacking Routine: 40-Hour Fasts, Cold Plunges, and Hyperbaric Oxygen
description: After a 2017 thyroid cancer diagnosis, Shark Tank's Daymond John now practices 40-hour weekly fasts, red-light therapy, cold plunges, and annual executive physicals at Fountain Life.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Profiles of 11 key companies including Altos Labs ($3B), Life Biosciences, NewLimit, and Genflow Biosciences, covering epigenetic reprogramming, gene therapy, and senolytic approaches.

title: Valter Longo to Present at Stanford on Fasting-Mimicking Diets and Longevity
description: Stanford Department of Medicine hosts USC gerontology professor Valter Longo to discuss fasting-mimicking diets and their role in transforming healthy aging research.

title: Eric Verdin (Buck Institute) on CD38 Inhibitors Extending Mouse Lifespan
description: Buck Institute president Eric Verdin presents data on CD38 inhibitor small molecules that correct NAD+ decline and extend lifespan in mouse models.

title: Novel Mechanism for Parkinson's Linked to ATP Deficiency in Synapses
description: Scientists discovered that ATP deficiency disrupts dopamine processing in synapses, leading to harmful protein accumulation that characterizes Parkinson's disease.

title: Polyamines: Longevity Booster or Cancer Fuel? Tokyo Researchers Reveal Dual Role
description: Tokyo University of Science researchers found polyamines have a dual nature -- boosting cellular cleanup and healthy aging in normal cells while turbocharging cancer growth in tumors.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic and NK Cell Advances
description: Monthly roundup highlights research in Nature Aging on mitochondrial stress enhancing senolytic effectiveness, plus work in Aging Cell on restoring aged natural killer cell function.

title: Rapamycin Low-Dose Study Reduces Senescence Marker p21 in Human Immune Cells
description: Research published in Aging Cell found that low-dose rapamycin significantly reduced p21, a marker of cellular senescence, in immune cells from older adults.

title: Norn Group: Industrializing Longevity with $34M in Research Grants
description: Martin Borchj Jensen describes Norn Group's effort to systematically fund longevity bottlenecks, including $34M in Impetus Grants and a clinical trial framework for aging.

title: Brown University Wins $22M ARPA-H Award for Censavudine Aging Trial
description: ARPA-H awards up to $22M for a five-year project testing Censavudine in 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65 to determine if the drug can slow human biological aging.

title: Loyal Raises $100M Series C to Advance First FDA Longevity Drug for Dogs
description: Loyal closed a $100M Series C to advance LOY-002 toward FDA approval. The STAY pivotal trial has enrolled 1,300+ dogs across 70+ clinics, making it the largest animal health trial ever.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Bets Senescent Cell Clearance Can Rejuvenate Skin
description: Rubedo's RLS-1496, a topical GPX4 modulator designed to push ferroptosis-sensitive senescent cells toward death, is in Phase 1 trials with a systemic formulation planned for 2026.

title: New Study Reveals 55% of Aging is Genetic, Challenging Decades of Assumptions
description: A landmark paper in Science using advanced twin-study models found genetics may account for 55% of lifespan variation, more than double the previously accepted 20-25% estimate.
